item.headline: Doing @ Thinking Machines Lab
item.topSkills: doing things • making stuff • talking about doing things and making stuff
item.about: Most of my time is spent on Thinking Machines, where I do technical programs and other things.

experience.description:
technical programs, ecosystem, strategy, culture, and product-y things at everyone's favourite AI research + product company. ~all of my time is spent on tinker, the world's best post-training API: https://thinkingmachines.ai/tinker

i previously ran our recruiting function. we're hiring! https://job-boards.greenhouse.io/thinkingmachines

Partnered with founders David Reilly and Thomas Ohki on Emergence's initial strategy and operations. EQ is a world-class contracting R&D org (in the style of BBN) that solves the unanticipated challenges for next-generation advanced computing---the kinds that only emerge as systems scale. 

Emergence works with defense, intelligence, and private organisations. Reach out if you have a need in hardcore electrical engineering for quantum or other compute modalities that needs a strike team.

experience.companyName: Department of Industry, Science and Resources
experience.description: Invited as one of fifteen "industry leaders" by the Hon Ed Husic, Minister for Science and Industry, and Dr Cathy Foley, Chief Scientist, to advise on Australia's first National Quantum Strategy.
Encour is a strategic communications agency that emboldens clients to command the narrative. Jessy let me work for her a day a week while I was "on sabbatical".

experience.position: Senior Investment Associate and "Scientist in Residence"
experience.description: Invested in frontier tech (quantum, medical devices, bio, fusion, photonics, solar abundance...) from pre-seed to Series E and created and led Foundry, a frontier-tech accelerator.

experience.position: Chemistry Research Assistant and Outreach Team Lead
Student Fellow to the Academic Board and the Faculty of Science and its Board
experience.description: Elected by peers to represent undergraduate interests at the University's second-highest governing body, and to represent student interests in science curriculum and student experience.
Coached high school students with specific learning needs.

education.description:
My Honours research contributed to the start of what became the first simulation of a chemical reaction on a quantum computer.
Thesis: Near-term analog quantum simulation of nonadiabatic chemical dynamics.
Supervisor: Prof Ivan Kassal
Publication: https://pubs.rsc.org/en/Content/ArticleLanding/2021/SC/D1SC02142G
education.insights: Grade: First Class with the University Medal, Rank 1 in School of Chemistry

education.description: Address of the Faculty Scholar, Hush Prize in Theoretical Chemistry, Chancellor's Scholar, Helen Beh Citizenship Award

Worked in A/Prof Jun Zhou's group towards optimising the peroxidase-mimicking biocatalytic activity of G-quadruplex/hemin DNAzymes, with some computational work exploring the role of electrostatics in the hemin binding pocket. Poster presentation at APATCC-9.

volunteering.description:
Supervised + lectured five undergrads in a surface chemistry/art citizen science project w Dr Alice Motion and A/Prof Chiara Neto.
Supervised + lectured five undergrads in investigating Lie algebras and representation theory w/r/t the Schrödinger equation, with Prof Ruibin Zhang.
Also was a mentor for first-year science students.

skills.name: talking about doing things and making stuff

publications.title: Analog quantum simulation of chemical dynamics
publications.description: Ultrafast chemical reactions are difficult to simulate because they involve entangled, many-body wavefunctions whose computational complexity grows rapidly with molecular size. In photochemistry, the breakdown of the Born-Oppenheimer approximation further complicates the problem by entangling nuclear and electronic degrees of freedom. Here, we show that analog quantum simulators can efficiently simulate molecular dynamics using commonly available bosonic modes to represent molecular vibrations. Our approach can be implemented in any device with a qudit controllably coupled to bosonic oscillators and with quantum hardware resources that scale linearly with molecular size, and with more than a ten-fold resource savings compared to digital quantum simulation algorithms. Advantages of our approach include a time resolution orders of magnitude better than ultrafast spectroscopy, the ability to simulate large molecules with limited hardware using a Suzuki-Trotter expansion, and the ability to implement realistic system-bath interactions with only one additional interaction per mode. Our approach can be implemented with current technology; e.g., the conical intersection in pyrazine can be simulated using a single trapped ion. Therefore, we expect our method will enable classically intractable chemical dynamics simulations in the near term.

honorsAndAwards.title: Eureka Prize for STEM Inclusion
honorsAndAwards.issuedBy: Australian Museum and Department of Industry, Science, Energy and Resources
honorsAndAwards.description:
The Eureka Awards are Australia's top national STEM awards.

The Department of Industry, Science, Energy and Resources Eureka Prize for STEM Inclusion is awarded for an initiative that has led to greater inclusion in science, technology, engineering and mathematics (STEM).

honorsAndAwards.title: 2021 Ripple x City of Sydney Emerging Civic Leader
honorsAndAwards.description: Selected as one of twenty Sydney young people passionate about leading social change to undertake a 6-week intensive, action-oriented program to learn to amplify youth and young adult voices in community decision-making.

honorsAndAwards.title: The University Medal
honorsAndAwards.description: The University Medal is awarded to honours students based on outstanding academic performance over their complete student enrolment. This includes your honours program and your undergraduate studies. You may be nominated for a medal by your discipline with a decision made by the Honours Board of Examiners.

honorsAndAwards.title: Address of the Faculty Scholar
honorsAndAwards.description: Invited opening address at the annual Faculty of Science Awards Night, 2019. Selected from all students in the Faculty.

honorsAndAwards.title: Hush Prize in Theoretical Chemistry
honorsAndAwards.description: Established in honour of Professor Noel Hush, this prize is awarded annually on the recommendation of the Head of the School of Chemistry for outstanding performance in Senior Chemistry by a student proceeding on to an Honours year in Theoretical Chemistry.

honorsAndAwards.title: Helen Beh Citizenship Award
honorsAndAwards.description: Established in 1997 in honour of Professor Helen Beh and her services to the Faculty of Science and the University of Sydney, this prize is awarded to an undergraduate student who has contributed significantly to the Faculty of Science’s non-academic activities and interests.

honorsAndAwards.title: University of Sydney Honours Scholarship
honorsAndAwards.description: Awarded on the basis of academic merit, curriculum vitae, and a personal statement which demonstrates their academic achievements, leadership experience, communication skills, relevant work experience and interest in studying in the proposed Honours Program, by the Academic Board on the recommendation of a committee consisting of various Faculty Deans, or their nominated delegates.

honorsAndAwards.title: Vice Chancellor's Mobility Scholarship
honorsAndAwards.description: The Nanjing Research Exchange offers current senior undergraduate students the opportunity to gain first–hand research experience in an international context as part of their degree program. Successful applicants travel to Nanjing University in China for 4 weeks and work under some of China’s leading scientific researchers in the disciplines of astronomy & astrophysics or chemistry.

honorsAndAwards.title: Maud Stiles Prize
honorsAndAwards.issuedBy: School of Letters, Arts, and Media
honorsAndAwards.description: Awarded for outstanding performance in first year English.

honorsAndAwards.title: University of Sydney Chancellor's Award
honorsAndAwards.description: Awarded for outstanding performance in Year 12.
